subject: UX & Research
title: The Healthcare.gov Collapse
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Healthcare.gov Collapse usable.

hook.text: October 1, 2013. Millions tried to sign up for health insurance. The site crashed within two hours.

buildup.text: Healthcare.gov forced users to create accounts before even browsing plans. A wall before the window.

discovery.text: The registration system couldn't handle the load. Users waited minutes for each page to respond.

twist.text: A rescue team rewrote the flow. Let people browse plans first, register later. Traffic soared, crashes stopped.

climax.text: The fix wasn't more servers. It was better UX: remove the gate, let users see value before committing.

punchline.text: Don't make users register before they see value.

quiz.question: What was Healthcare.gov's core UX mistake?
options: Forcing account creation before users could browse plans
Using too many colors on the homepage
